title: Turrillia lutea
intro: Turrillia lutea, also known as the yellow Turrillia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Turrillia lutea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Turrillia lutea include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support joint health and immune function.
taxonomy: Turrillia lutea is classified in the plant family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Turrillia lutea specifically belonging to the subfamily Aurantioideae. The Aurantioideae subfamily consists of plants native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Turrillia lutea is a flowering plant species native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in the Indian Ocean. The classification of Turrillia lutea within the Rutaceae family reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
